explanation: To translate the requirement 'The self-test procedure shall confirm the oxygen sensor is calibrated' into LTL: First, identify the atomic propositions representing self-test completion and oxygen sensor success. 'selfTestPass' indicates all self-test steps (including oxygen sensor) passed successfully, and 'testOxygenSensorPass' represents oxygen sensor passed the test. The requirement implies that whenever the entire self-test process passes, it must be the case that the oxygen sensor test has also passed at some point beforehand or simultaneously. This becomes: whenever selfTestPass occurs, testOxygenSensorPass must have occurred previously or at the same time. As LTL does not directly provide 'previously', but implication 'if selfTestPass then testOxygenSensorPass must hold in the current state or earlier' can be encoded as: globally, selfTestPass can only be true if testOxygenSensorPass has already been true at some point, i.e., G(selfTestPass -> P testOxygenSensorPass). LTL, as requested, does not have 'P' (previous), so we must use the available future temporal operators. Rewriting: Whenever selfTestPass holds, then testOxygenSensorPass must have occurred previously. In standard LTL, this is approximated by stating that selfTestPass cannot be true unless testOxygenSensorPass has been true in the past, or that selfTestPass never happens before testOxygenSensorPass, i.e., testOxygenSensorPass must occur before or at the same time as selfTestPass. Thus: 'It is always the case that if selfTestPass occurs, then testOxygenSensorPass has already occurred (at or before this point)' can be encoded (with LTL over future operators only) as: testOxygenSensorPass must occur before or at the same time as selfTestPass; this is usually written as: !(selfTestPass && !testOxygenSensorPass) U testOxygenSensorPass. But to formalize: globally, it is not possible for selfTestPass to occur before testOxygenSensorPass. The standard LTL for this relation is: G(testOxygenSensorPass W selfTestPass), meaning 'testOxygenSensorPass must hold at least until selfTestPass holds (and if selfTestPass never holds, no constraint)' or equivalently: It is never the case that selfTestPass is true before testOxygenSensorPass is true. Alternatively, G(selfTestPass -> testOxygenSensorPass) (selfTestPass can only happen if testOxygenSensorPass is true in the same instant) is a stricter, but usable, encoding if we assume they appear in the same computation step. Thus, our primary formula is: G(selfTestPass -> testOxygenSensorPass).
